summary: Machine policy for the Windows location feature. Good for strict privacy or locked-down environments, but it blocks programs on the computer from using location information from the location feature.

setting.name: Location Feature
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can provide location information to apps and system features. This policy decides whether the location feature is turned off for the computer.
setting.why_people_change_it: People turn it off for privacy, compliance, or to make sure the device does not expose location data.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning it off improves privacy and reduces location exposure, but maps, weather, nearby-device features, and other location-aware experiences can stop working correctly.
setting.professional_notes: Sensors.admx defines both user and machine variants of DisableLocation. The current app writes the machine policy at HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors\DisableLocation with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows does not force the location feature off for the computer.

allowed_values.label: Location allowed
allowed_values.meaning: The policy is disabled and programs are not prevented from using location information.

allowed_values.label: Location turned off
allowed_values.meaning: The location feature is turned off and programs on the computer are prevented from using location information from that feature.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes DisableLocation = 1 under the machine policy key, which matches the documented turned-off state.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 7 and newer systems exposing the LocationAndSensors policy

states.rationale: The machine policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: If the policy is disabled or not configured, programs are not prevented from using location information from the location feature.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Devices that rely on location-aware features
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want location-aware features to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to prevent location usage across the machine.
tradeoffs: Location-aware apps and features can keep working.
The device does not get a policy-based location block.

recommended_profiles.label: No location
avoid_for: Users who need maps, weather, or other location-aware features

states.rationale: Use the documented machine-policy off state.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to make sure the Windows location feature is turned off.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You depend on features that expect location information.
tradeoffs: Programs using the Windows location feature can stop working correctly.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Sensors.admx DisableLocation mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines both user and machine DisableLocation policies under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors with enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft Sensors.adml DisableLocation help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text says enabling the policy turns off the location feature and prevents all programs on the computer from using location information from that feature.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes DisableLocation = 1 under the machine policy key for the current tweak.

decision.why: Local Microsoft policy files document the exact machine policy surface, value semantics, and location-feature behavior. The app writes the documented turned-off value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: <policy name="DisableLocation_2" class="Machine" ... key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\LocationAndSensors" valueName="DisableLocation">; Sensors.adml: If you enable this policy setting, the location feature is turned off, and all programs on this computer are prevented from using location information from the location feature.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact machine-policy key and value name; local ADML confirms that enabling the policy turns off the Windows location feature for the computer.
